whatIs: GnRH Triptorelin is a synthetic decapeptide GnRH agonist that suppresses pituitary gonadotropin secretion through receptor downregulation.
mechanism: Initially stimulates then suppresses LH/FSH via GnRH receptor downregulation.
ref1: Conn PM et al. N Engl J Med 1991

whatIs: Gonadorelin is synthetic GnRH a decapeptide that regulates pituitary LH and FSH secretion through pulsatile signalling.
mechanism: Binds GnRH receptors on pituitary gonadotrophs stimulating pulsatile LH/FSH release.
ref2: Conn PM et al. Endocr Rev 1986

whatIs: HCG is a glycoprotein hormone that acts as an LH analog stimulating gonadal steroidogenesis.
mechanism: Binds to LH/HCG receptors on gonadal cells activating cAMP signalling to stimulate testosterone production.
ref1: Stenman UH et al. Clin Chem 2006
ref2: Choi J et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2005

whatIs: HGH 191AA is recombinant human growth hormone identical to endogenous pituitary GH a 191-amino acid protein regulating growth and metabolism.
mechanism: Binds to GH receptors activating JAK2/STAT5 signalling stimulating IGF-1 production.
ref1: Moller N et al. Endocr Rev 2009

whatIs: HGH-97 is a 97-amino acid N-terminal fragment of human growth hormone retaining some metabolic activity.
mechanism: Binds GH receptors with lower affinity than full-length HGH retaining some metabolic effects.
ref1: Chawla RK et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 1982

whatIs: IGF-1 DES is a truncated analogue of IGF-1 lacking the N-terminal tripeptide making it more potent.
mechanism: Binds IGF-1 receptors with reduced affinity for IGFBPs increasing bioavailability.
ref1: Tomas FM et al. Biochem J 1991
ref2: Francis GL et al. J Mol Endocrinol 1992

whatIs: IGF-1 LR3 is an 83-amino acid recombinant analog of human IGF-1 with prolonged half-life.
mechanism: The Arg3 substitution reduces IGFBP binding while maintaining potent IGF-1 receptor activation.
ref1: Tomas FM et al. Biochem J 1991
ref2: Francis GL et al. J Endocrinol 2008

whatIs: KLOW is an enhanced peptide blend combining GHK-Cu BPC-157 TB-500 and KPV for comprehensive tissue repair.
mechanism: GHK-Cu signals collagen synthesis BPC-157 promotes angiogenesis TB-500 modulates cell migration KPV provides anti-inflammatory activity.
ref1: Pickart L et al. J Biomater Sci 2012

whatIs: L-Carnitine is a naturally occurring amino acid derivative that transports fatty acids into mitochondria for energy production.
mechanism: Acts as a carrier molecule shuttling fatty acyl groups across the inner mitochondrial membrane via CPT system.
ref1: Rebouche CJ et al. Annu Rev Nutr 1998

whatIs: Lipo-C is a lipotropic injectable blend of methionine inositol choline and B-vitamins supporting liver fat metabolism.
mechanism: Methionine donates methyl groups for lipid transport inositol facilitates fat movement choline supports VLDL synthesis.
ref1: Bell SJ et al. Altern Med Rev 2000

whatIs: LL-37 is the only human cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide with broad-spectrum antimicrobial and wound-healing properties.
mechanism: Disrupts bacterial membranes and modulates immune responses through formyl peptide receptor 2 FPR2 signalling.
ref2: Kahlenberg JM et al. Am J Pathol 2013

whatIs: Matrixyl 3000 is a cosmetic peptide blend of pal-GHK and pal-GQPR for anti-aging skincare.
mechanism: Pal-GHK stimulates collagen via TGF-beta signalling pal-GQPR reduces IL-6 mediated inflammation.
ref1: Lintner K et al. Cosmet Toiletries 2002

whatIs: Melatonin is a naturally occurring pineal hormone that regulates the circadian sleep-wake cycle and has antioxidant properties.
mechanism: Binds to MT1 and MT2 receptors in the suprachiasmatic nucleus regulating circadian entrainment and scavenging free radicals.
ref1: Brzezinski A N Engl J Med 1997

whatIs: MGF is a splice variant of IGF-1 produced in response to mechanical stress promoting local tissue repair.
mechanism: Produced locally in response to muscle strain activating satellite cells through distinct signalling from systemic IGF-1.
ref2: Yang SY et al. J Anat 2010

whatIs: MK-677 is an orally active non-peptide GH secretagogue that mimics ghrelin at the GHSR-1a receptor.
mechanism: Binds selectively to ghrelin receptors stimulating GH pulse amplitude without affecting cortisol or prolactin.
ref1: Murphy MG et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 1998
ref2: Copinschi G et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 1997

whatIs: MT-1 is a synthetic alpha-MSH analog that stimulates melanogenesis for photoprotection FDA-approved for EPP.
mechanism: Binds to MC1R on melanocytes activating cAMP to stimulate eumelanin production.
ref1: Hoff NP et al. Br J Dermatol 2003
ref2: Barnard J et al. Br J Dermatol 2010

whatIs: MT-2 is a synthetic cyclic alpha-MSH analog that stimulates melanogenesis more potent than MT-1.
mechanism: Binds MC1R with higher potency inducing eumelanin with additional effects on MC3R and MC4R.
ref1: Hadley ME et al. Ann N Y Acad Sci 1993
ref2: Rosenberg DB et al. J Drugs Dermatol 2004

whatIs: Myostatin is an endogenous TGF-beta member that negatively regulates muscle growth.
mechanism: Binds to ActRIIB activating SMAD2/3 suppressing MyoD and limiting muscle fibre hypertrophy.
ref2: Lee SJ Annu Rev Cell Dev Biol 2004
